source_prompt: $keelplane proceed on making figure-agent visibly meaningful after the fig3 composition-family dogfood slice

activation.reason: The next useful slice spans CLI contracts, generated candidate artifacts, rendered PNG evidence, review packets, source/apply safety gates, and independent review roles. It needs resumable handoffs and visual evidence gates rather than a single direct edit.

objective: Turn the current fig3 composition-family work into a visible, evidence-backed improvement loop: generate structural and composite candidates, render comparison boards, attach deterministic visual evidence to ranking/review packets, and preserve hash-bound human acceptance/apply gates.

assumptions.claim: The current structural-family slice can already generate and render at least six fig3 candidates plus a refined composite comparison board.
assumptions.verification: Find candidate_set.json, render manifests, composition_review_packet.json files, and fig3_*comparison.png artifacts under the fig3 build/candidates directory.

assumptions.claim: The system is meaningful only if the rendered visual difference is inspectable without manually reading TeX diffs.
assumptions.verification: Review packet JSON must point to rendered candidate artifacts and a comparison board; tests must fail if these paths are omitted or stale.

assumptions.claim: No plugin-side model/API calls are allowed in this loop.
assumptions.verification: Static grep and focused review of touched composition scripts must show no network/model client invocation.

assumptions.claim: Fixture source mutation remains gated by existing P6/P7 acceptance/apply artifacts.
assumptions.verification: compose-apply-ready, compose-accept, and compose-apply tests must still pass, and no new command may replace fixture source outside compose-apply.

phases.name: Freeze Current Visual Baseline
entry_criteria: Known unrelated dirty files are preserved
exit_criteria: baseline_state.json names candidate_set hash, best candidate, board paths, and verification commands
No unrelated dirty file was modified

phases.name: Review Packet Carries Visible Evidence
exit_criteria: composition_review_packet.json can be opened as a complete visual decision packet
Tests prove rendered evidence paths exist and are hash-associated

phases.name: Render-Derived Visual Signals
entry_criteria: P1 review packet includes visible evidence
exit_criteria: Rank/review can cite visual_metrics.json as evidence without treating it as an aesthetic oracle

phases.name: Composite Search Loop
entry_criteria: At least three single-family rendered candidates exist
exit_criteria: At least one composite candidate renders and appears in a comparison board
Rank output explains why the composite is preferable or rejects it with evidence

phases.name: Judge Panel And Refutation
entry_criteria: P3 has a candidate board and rank result
exit_criteria: review_synthesis.md contains findings-first status with evidence paths

phases.name: Human Acceptance And Apply Boundary
entry_criteria: P4 review_synthesis.md has no unresolved hard refutations
A human explicitly selects a candidate
exit_criteria: Accepted/applied artifacts are hash-bound, or the workflow stops safely before source mutation

phases.name: Post-Apply Proof
entry_criteria: P5 applied a candidate through compose-apply
exit_criteria: The applied source has visible before/after proof and a rollback patch
The final report states whether TeX/export/golden artifacts were or were not refreshed

phases.name: Generalize Beyond Fig3
exit_criteria: A second fixture can emit rendered creative candidates or a precise missing-prerequisite report

workers.role: Map current repo, artifact, and verification state before further edits.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: baseline_state.json with repo_status, artifact_hashes, best_candidate, commands_run, and blocked_items
stop_conditions: unrelated dirty file would be modified
required artifact is missing and cannot be regenerated without TeX approval

workers.role: Attach rendered visual artifacts and deterministic image checks to review outputs.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: visual_metrics.json plus review packet fields for visual_evidence
stop_conditions: review packet cannot prove artifact existence
implementation would require new image dependency

workers.role: Build bounded composite candidate generation on top of existing operation contracts.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: composite candidate-set JSON with component_candidate_ids and bounded operations
stop_conditions: composite operation overlaps source blocks incompatibly

workers.role: Refute claims against source, render artifacts, tests, and safety gates.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: review_synthesis.md with confirmed, refuted, unverified, and required fixes
stop_conditions: artifact referenced in packet is missing

workers.role: Run focused verification and report exact pass/fail evidence.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: verification_receipt.json with commands, exit codes, summaries, and skipped checks

parallelism.fan_in_rule: P4 fan-in synthesizes reviewer outputs; P5 cannot start until hard refutations are either fixed or explicitly accepted as stop conditions.
barriers: P5 human acceptance before source mutation

verification.claim_or_output: Review packets are now visible decision packets, not JSON-only summaries.
verification.falsifier: A composition_review_packet.json for a rendered candidate lacks render artifact paths, comparison board paths, or hash linkage.
evidence_required: Real fig3 review packet path and file-existence check

verification.claim_or_output: Visual metrics are deterministic evidence only.
verification.falsifier: visual_metrics.json contains subjective aesthetic scores or blocks candidates without a deterministic artifact check.
evidence_required: Code review of visual metrics generation

verification.claim_or_output: Composite search preserves sandbox and source-mutation boundaries.
verification.falsifier: A composite command writes fixture source or bypasses compose-render/compose-apply contracts.
evidence_required: Focused pytest for composite generation, render, and review

verification.claim_or_output: No plugin-side model/API calls were introduced.
verification.falsifier: Touched plugin code imports or invokes network/model client APIs.
evidence_required: Static grep over touched composition scripts

verification.claim_or_output: The loop produces a visibly inspectable figure improvement candidate.
verification.falsifier: No comparison PNG exists, image is blank, or candidate render paths are missing.

risk_gates.trigger: Any filesystem write to repository, fixture, or generated artifact paths.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before writing files and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Any shell command, including pytest, ruff, py_compile, TeX render, or artifact inspection.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before running shell commands and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Installing a new dependency for image metrics or rendering.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before installing dependency and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Running TeX/render beyond the explicitly selected fixture or beyond existing render commands.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before rendering selected fixture and ask for approval
Stop before mutating source and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Writing accepted, golden, or export artifacts.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before writing accepted golden export artifacts and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Pushing commits or publishing results externally.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before pushing commits and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Adding plugin-side model/API calls or MCP surfaces.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before adding model api mcp surfaces and ask for approval

budget.file_touch_limit: Up to 8 source files, 6 test files, 3 docs files, and 4 fixture files; generated candidate artifacts are capped separately by wave receipts.

first_slice.instruction: Create a baseline visual evidence receipt for the current fig3 composition-family state without changing source behavior: inventory candidate artifacts, select the current best candidate, record board paths, and add no implementation code unless a missing receipt writer is strictly necessary.
first_slice.expected_output: baseline_state.json plus a short receipt naming comparison boards, best candidate, artifact hashes, and verification commands.
first_slice.completion_check: python -m json.tool validates baseline_state.json, referenced board files exist, and git status shows no unrelated file touched.

slices.instruction: Inventory current fig3 candidate artifacts and write baseline_state.json with hashes, board paths, best candidate id, current verification status, and protected dirty files.
slices.completion_check: JSON validates, all referenced paths exist, and no unrelated dirty file changes.

slices.instruction: Add RED tests proving review packets for rendered candidates must include visual evidence paths and hash linkage, without changing production code in the same slice.
slices.expected_output: Failing focused test that specifies visual_evidence contract.
slices.completion_check: Focused pytest fails for the new expected visual_evidence fields only.

first_wave.entry_gate: P0 current state is understood and fig3 fixture remains the only promoted visual dogfood fixture.
first_wave.exit_gate: baseline receipt exists and the visual_evidence contract has a red test or documented blocker.
first_wave.fan_in: Use the baseline receipt to decide the minimal production changes for W2.

slices: P1 review packet carries visible evidence
waves.entry_gate: W1 exit receipt verifies current artifact paths and the visual evidence RED test is present.
waves.exit_gate: Focused tests pass and a real CFAM_COMBO_002 review packet names valid visible evidence.

waves.entry_gate: W2 verification receipt confirms visible evidence and no model/API calls.
waves.exit_gate: Composite candidate board, rank result, and review_synthesis.md exist with no hard unresolved refutations.

slices: P5 human acceptance and apply boundary
waves.entry_gate: W3 receipt verified and user explicitly chooses either apply the selected fig3 candidate or skip apply and generalize.
waves.exit_gate: Hash-bound apply proof exists or second-fixture prerequisite report exists.
